stories.title: Fasando Baby Lounger CPSC Urgent Warning — Infant Death Reported

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: CPSC urgent warning: Stop using Fasando Baby Loungers immediately. A 2-month-old died in 2025 after being placed to sleep in one. ~6,200 units sold on Amazon. The company has refused to recall. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: If you have a Fasando Baby Lounger, act now. Remove the foam, cut the cover in half, and dispose of it. The CPSC issued an urgent warning after an infant death — and the company refuses to recall. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
🚨 If you have a Fasando Baby Lounger, stop using it today.

The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has issued an urgent warning after a 2-month-old infant died in 2025 following a sleep session in a Fasando Baby Lounger placed on top of an adult bed. The CPSC found that the lounger violates the mandatory standard for Infant Sleep Products — the sides are too low to safely contain an infant, and the foot openings are wider than allowed, creating fall and entrapment hazards.

Approximately 6,200 units were sold on Amazon.com between August 2024 and July 2025 for about $35. The loungers came in grey elephant, baby blue animals, blue animals, gold elephant, pink lion, and yellow elephant patterns. The company, Fasando — operating out of China — has NOT agreed to recall or offer any remedy. That means the CPSC cannot force a refund, but the warning is firm: these products should not be in use.

Here is what to do right now if you have one: stop using it immediately, remove the foam padding from the cover, cut the cover in half, and dispose of both the cover and the foam. Do not donate it, resell it, or pass it on.

The CPSC's official warning is available at cpsc.gov. If you're unsure whether your lounger is the Fasando brand, check the product tag or your Amazon order history from that period.

Have you come across any products that were flagged for safety concerns after you purchased them? What did you do?

---

#### Image Caption -- 8:00 AM MT
facebook.image_caption: 🚨 CPSC urgent warning: Fasando Baby Loungers are linked to an infant death and violate federal safety standards — but the company refuses to recall them. If you have one, stop using it now and follow the CPSC's disposal steps.

article.headline: CPSC Urgent Warning: Stop Using Fasando Baby Loungers — Infant Death Reported

stories.title: Two April 16 Toy Recalls — ATOYUS Activity Cubes and Sanlebi Pet Vet Playsets

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Two toy recalls announced April 16 — both sold on Amazon, both a hazard for young children. ATOYUS Activity Cubes (choking hazard) and Sanlebi Pet Vet Playsets (button cell batteries). Check your toy boxes. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Reminder: two Amazon toy recalls from April 16 still need action. ATOYUS Activity Cubes and Sanlebi Pet Vet Playsets — both have hazards for young kids. Details and refund steps in today's post. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Two toy recalls to check today — both announced April 16, both sold on Amazon, and both a real hazard for young children.

🧸 First: ATOYUS Children's Activity Cubes. These are intended for kids under 3, which makes the hazard especially serious. The xylophone screws and clock hands can detach and become small parts, and the mallets have spherical ends that violate the federal small parts ban. If you have this product, write "recalled" on all sides of the cube, cut the playmat in half, and email a photo to ATOYUSActivityCubeRecall@outlook.com for a refund. The cube was sold by Melofaver US on Amazon.

🐾 Second: Sanlebi Pet Vet Playsets. These playsets include a nail grinder and an otoscope that contain button cell batteries — and young children can easily access them. Button cell batteries are a serious hazard if swallowed, causing rapid internal injuries. To get a refund or replacement, remove the nail grinder and otoscope from the set, take out the batteries, and send a photo to letorecall@outlook.com. The playset was sold by Letokids on Amazon.

Both of these recalls were posted to the CPSC's official site at cpsc.gov on April 16, 2026. If you've purchased either of these products for a child under 6, check your toy box today.

Have you had to deal with a toy recall before? Was the refund process straightforward?

---

#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM MT
facebook.image_caption: 🧸 Two toy recalls announced April 16 — both on Amazon, both posing serious risks to young children. ATOYUS Activity Cubes (choking hazard) and Sanlebi Pet Vet Playsets (button cell battery access) need immediate attention. Check the post for refund steps.

article.headline: Two Amazon Toy Recalls Announced April 16: ATOYUS Activity Cubes and Sanlebi Pet Vet Playsets

stories.title: Elevate Trampoline Park Opens in Colorado Springs — April 18

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: New this weekend in COS: Elevate Trampoline Park opens Saturday, April 18, at its new Colorado Springs location. The 31,000-square-foot indoor activity center is offering guaranteed prizes for the first 250 guests! #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
COS families, there's a new spot opening this Saturday. 🎉

Elevate Trampoline Park is opening a brand-new Colorado Springs location on April 18, 2026, with a grand opening event that comes with some solid perks. The facility is 31,000 square feet of indoor activity space — trampolines, games, and active recreation for families and groups.

The first 250 guests to arrive will receive guaranteed prizes. The opening also features discounted founders memberships and promotional birthday party packages for families interested in locking in a deal at launch.

If you've been looking for a new indoor activity option heading into spring and summer, this one is worth checking out this weekend. Colorado Springs has a handful of trampoline parks, and a new location adds more capacity and options for the region.

Have you been to an Elevate Trampoline Park before? What do your kids think of trampoline parks compared to other indoor activity venues?

---

stories.title: AAP Research — Your Neighborhood Shapes Your Child's Crash Risk

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: New AAP research: where your child lives shapes their pedestrian crash risk — not just their behavior. The study examines walkability, traffic density, and neighborhood conditions that create unequal safety environments. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
A new blog published in Pediatrics — the American Academy of Pediatrics' journal — is asking a question that's worth sitting with: is your child's neighborhood making them less safe?

The article, "Safe Passage: How Neighborhoods Influence Pediatric Crash Risk," published April 16, 2026, examines the community-level factors that shape how likely children are to be involved in a pedestrian or traffic crash. The research looks at things like walkability, traffic density, lighting, and socioeconomic conditions — and finds that these structural factors create meaningfully different risk environments for children depending on where they live.

This matters because most pedestrian safety messaging is aimed at individual behavior — look both ways, use crosswalks, don't use your phone. That's all still important. But this research suggests that the neighborhood a child grows up in shapes their crash risk in ways that individual behavior alone can't fully address.

For families in Colorado Springs, that's worth thinking about. Not every neighborhood has the same sidewalk quality, crosswalk frequency, or traffic calming infrastructure. The routes kids walk to school, to a friend's house, or to a park are part of their safety picture too.

What's the pedestrian safety situation like in your part of COS — is it something you think about when your kids are out on their own?

---

#### Image Caption -- 3:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: 🚶 New AAP-published research finds that where your child lives shapes their pedestrian crash risk — independent of behavior. The "Safe Passage" study examines how walkability, traffic density, and neighborhood conditions create unequal safety environments for children.

article.headline: New AAP Research: Where Your Child Lives Shapes Their Pedestrian Crash Risk

stories.title: D20 Approves Pay Raises Across the Board for Teachers

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Academy School District 20 has approved pay raises for all teachers. The district's minimum starting salary is now $52,020 — one of the higher floors in the Pikes Peak region. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Academy School District 20 has approved pay raises for teachers across the board, according to KOAA.

The raises push D20's minimum starting teacher salary to $52,020, putting the district among the higher starting-pay floors in the Pikes Peak region. For comparison, D11's minimum starting pay is $51,510. The gap isn't enormous, but in a competitive teacher market, even small differences in compensation can influence where teachers choose to take jobs and whether they stay.

D20 serves families in northern Colorado Springs and is one of three major districts — alongside D11 and D49 — that together serve most COS families. Teacher compensation has been a recurring challenge across Colorado as districts navigate budget pressures while trying to retain experienced educators. Approving across-the-board raises signals that D20 is treating recruitment and retention as a priority this budget cycle.

For families in D20, more competitive compensation generally means the district is better positioned to attract qualified candidates and reduce the kind of teacher turnover that disrupts student learning.

What matters most to you when it comes to your child's school district making budget and staffing decisions?

---

#### Image Caption -- 5:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: 📚 Academy School District 20 has approved pay raises for all teachers, setting the district's minimum starting salary at $52,020. The raises are aimed at helping D20 recruit and retain teachers in the Colorado Springs market.

article.headline: Academy School District 20 Approves Pay Raises Across the Board for Teachers
